# Ryohei Mogi / 茂木良平

> Demographer and researcher specializing in low fertility, population decline,
> family demography, and evidence-based policy (EBPM).
> Beatriu de Pinós Fellow at Pompeu Fabra University, Barcelona.
> Policy advisor to Japanese municipalities on population issues.

## Who I am

Ryohei Mogi (茂木良平, もぎ りょうへい) is a Japanese demographer based in Barcelona, Spain.
He is a Beatriu de Pinós Fellow at the Department of Political and Social Sciences,
Pompeu Fabra University. His research focuses on fertility, union formation,
family demography, and population policy in Japan and cross-nationally.

He bridges academic research with real-world policy action, working with
Japanese municipal governments, national ministries, and media outlets.

## Expertise

- Low fertility and demographic decline (少子化・人口減少)
- Family demography: fertility, marriage, union formation
- Evidence-based policy making (EBPM / エビデンスに基づく政策立案)
- Japan's population policy
- Cross-national comparative demography (Japan, Europe, East Asia)
- Data visualization and statistical analysis (R, Python, Stata)

## Current positions

- Beatriu de Pinós Fellow, Pompeu Fabra University (2025–present)
- Visiting Research Fellow, National Institute of Population and Social Security Research, Japan (2020–present)
- Population Policy Advisor, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan (2024–present)
- Childcare Survey Advisor, Fukui Prefecture, Japan (2023–present)

## Selected publications

- Mogi, R., Mugiyama, R., & Raybould, A. (2025). Exposure to non-partnership and fertility desires among childless population in Japan. *Social Forces*. DOI: 10.1093/sf/soaf123
- Mogi, R. et al. (2023). An alternative version of the second demographic transition? Changing pathways to first marriage in Japan. *Demographic Research*, 49(16). DOI: 10.4054/DemRes.2023.49.16
- Mogi, R., Nisén, J., & Canudas-Romo, V. (2021). Cross-sectional average length of life childless. *Demography*, 58(1), 321–344. DOI: 10.1215/00703370-8937427
- Mogi, R. & Arpino, B. (2024). Is intending to have children rightist? *Statistics, Politics and Policy*, 15(2). DOI: 10.1515/spp-2023-0038 [Emil Julius Gumbel Prize]
- Fukuda, S., Yoda, S., & Mogi, R. (2021). Educational assortative mating in Japan. *Journal of Population Studies*, 57. [Best Article Award, Population Association of Japan]

## Awards

- 2025: Emil Julius Gumbel Prize, Statistics, Politics and Policy
- 2024: Best Article Award, Population Association of Japan
- 2024: Poster Award, Population Association of America
- 2023: Poster Award, Population Association of America
- 2021: Special Award for Doctoral Studies, Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona

## Media

Regular contributor to Nikkei Glocal (日経グローカル).
Quoted/featured in: Financial Times, Bloomberg Japan, Asahi Shimbun, Nikkei Shimbun,
Forbes Japan, Japan Times, rtve (Spain), TV2 (Denmark), Berlingske (Denmark), Weekly Playboy, VERY.

## Japanese summary / 日本語概要

茂木良平（もぎ りょうへい）は、少子化・人口減少を専門とする人口学者。
スペイン・バルセロナのポンペウ・ファブラ大学研究員（ベアトリウ・デ・ピノスフェロー）。
佐賀市人口政策アドバイザー、福井県子育て意識調査アドバイザー。
こども家庭庁・デジタル庁・厚生労働省などへの有識者ヒアリング対応実績あり。
日経グローカル連載（少子化統計の読み方シリーズ全6回、欧州少子化事情シリーズ全6回）。
専門は家族人口学・出生力・婚姻行動の国際比較・EBPM（エビデンスに基づく政策立案）。
1989年生まれ、埼玉県出身。二児の父。
